publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-09-17 00:10:10 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Diaries, correspondence, and personal and professional files of Anne Julienne Russ, with the diaries and correspondence of some members of her family: her mother Doris Russ, her grandmother Persons, her grandmother Russ, and her great grandmother Lotridge. Doris Russ' diaries include passages about dealing with her cancer, and Anne's papers include personal reflections on the death of her mother. Also, several yearbooks, including: her high school yearbook, the Uptonian (Mount Upton, NY), where she was class president, plus the '54 Uptonian showing her mother, Doris Russ, an English teacher and librarian. The Anne Russ diaries include reflections on many aspects of her life, reading, work, and friends and her delight in her daughter.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of sociology and director of secondary education program at Wells College and mother of Dorinne Barbara Russ. Her teaching interests included the sociology of education, Latin America, and women's studies. She explored the ways ethnicity, class, and gender influcence organizations and institutions. Her education included a B.S., State University of New York at Cortland, M.A., Southern Methodist University, and Ph.D., Cornell University. Born July 21, 1937, Russ died from cancer on March 5, 2001.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Diaries, correspondence, and personal and professional files of Anne Julienne Russ, with the diaries and correspondence of some members of her family: her mother Doris Russ, her grandmother Persons, her grandmother Russ, and her great grandmother Lotridge. Doris Russ' diaries include passages about dealing with her cancer, and Anne's papers include personal reflections on the death of her mother. Also, several yearbooks, including: her high school yearbook, the Uptonian (Mount Upton, NY), where she was class president, plus the '54 Uptonian showing her mother, Doris Russ, an English teacher and librarian. The Anne Russ diaries include reflections on many aspects of her life, reading, work, and friends and her delight in her daughter.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Persons and Russ family papers, #6435. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Essays by Anne (about her Grandpa and Mom); news article (about Dick Russ)
Adoption and Naturalization of Dorinne Barbara Russ
Correspondence: Doris Letters home from Syracuse University
Correspondence: Doris' letters home from Syracuse and first years of marriage in Ithaca
Letters to Grandparents (Mr. and Mrs. C.B. Persons) from Anne and Dicky
Anne Russ Philosophy of Education Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NYC visitor info / Russ coat of arms/ historiography / Lincoln Center info

did.unittitle: Bennet High School Commencement Program (Buffalo, NY)
"The Did"- Cortland State Teachers College Yearbook (NY)
"Tales from Grimm" book, translated by Wanda Gag

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Texas Drivers license and other miscellaneous papers

did.unittitle: Diary; Letters from George in summer 1978; miscellaneous letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed: Mexico trip notebook and book of poetry by Gowin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tan with pink and blue flowers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: London trip- black and white cityscape design

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous letters- many to Anne, and section of letters from Anne in Hawaii to her family. Also, misc. photos and documents (Anne's 6th grade report card and later test scores)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwriting appears to match 1905 and 1906 diaries

did.unittitle: "Wedding Token" (marriage of Sara Lotridge and Elijah Russ)
Diary - Mrs. E.D. Russ (Sara Lotridge- Anne's Great Grandmother)
Diary - Sara Lotridge (Anne's Great Grandmother- Mrs. Richard Lotridge?)
List of possessions- Mrs. C. B. Persons (Anne's Grandmother)
Diary - Mrs. C. B. Persons (Anne's Grandmother)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Births and development of children, Dicky and Anne
